revision.text: #redirect[[en:Shiragiyama_Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''白髪山古墳'''（しらがやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]西浦6丁目にあり、[[古市古墳群]]の南西端に位置する[[前方後円墳]]。'''清寧天皇陵'''とも。陵名は'''河内坂門原陵'''（かわちのさかどはらのみささぎ）で、第22代[[清寧天皇]]の[[陵墓]]に治定されている。
==概要==
白髪山古墳は、墳丘の長さ115メートル、前方部の幅128メートル・高さ11メートル、後円部は直径63メートル・高さ10.5メートルの前方後円墳である。 この古墳は、前方部の幅が後円部の幅の約2倍あり、前方部が大きいのが特徴である。
出土した円筒埴輪の破片の特徴から、6世紀前半に築造されたものと考えられている。
宮内庁によって清寧天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が前方部正面の濠の外にある。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=34/32/43.406&el=135/36/10.995&scl=25000&bid=Mlink 地図（mapionより）] *[http://maps.google.com/maps?ie=UTF8&om=1&z=18&ll=34.548948,135.6003&spn=0.002041,0.003074&t=k google.mapsの衛星画像]
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
[[category:古墳|しらがやまこふん]] [[category:日本の陵墓|しらがやまこふん]] [[category:羽曳野市|しらがやまこふん]]

revision.text:
'''白髪山古墳'''（しらがやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]西浦6丁目にあり、[[古市古墳群]]の南西端に位置する[[前方後円墳]]。'''清寧天皇陵'''とも。陵名は'''河内坂門原陵'''（かわちのさかどはらのみささぎ）で、第22代[[清寧天皇]]の[[陵墓]]に治定されている。
==概要==
白髪山古墳は、墳丘の長さ115メートル、前方部の幅128メートル・高さ11メートル、後円部は直径63メートル・高さ10.5メートルの前方後円墳である。 この古墳は、前方部の幅が後円部の幅の約2倍あり、前方部が大きいのが特徴である。
この古墳の名称は、被葬者とされる清寧天皇が生まれながら[[白髪]]であり、白髪大倭根子命（しらがのおおやまとねこのみこと：古事記）と名づけられていたことに由来する。
出土した円筒埴輪の破片の特徴から、6世紀前半に築造されたものと考えられている。
宮内庁によって清寧天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が前方部正面の濠の外にある。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=34/32/43.406&el=135/36/10.995&scl=25000&bid=Mlink 地図（mapionより）] *[http://maps.google.com/maps?ie=UTF8&om=1&z=18&ll=34.548948,135.6003&spn=0.002041,0.003074&t=k google.mapsの衛星画像]

revision.comment: 陵墓に係る自身の編集を修正、および陵墓項の序文表記統一。
revision.text:
'''白髪山古墳'''（しらがやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]西浦6丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の南西端に位置する。「'''清寧天皇陵'''」とも。
実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内坂門原陵'''（こうちのさかどのはらのみささぎ）」として第22代[[清寧天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。
== 概要 == 白髪山古墳は、墳丘の長さ115m、前方部の幅128m・高さ11m、後円部は直径63m・高さ10.5mの前方後円墳である。 この古墳は、前方部の幅が後円部の幅の約2倍あり、前方部が大きいのが特徴である。
この古墳の名称は、被葬者とされる清寧天皇が生まれながら[[白髪]]であり、白髪大倭根子命（しらがのおおやまとねこのみこと：[[古事記]]）と名づけられていたことに由来する。
出土した円筒埴輪の破片の特徴から、[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたものと考えられている。
[[宮内庁]]によって清寧天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が前方部正面の濠の外にある。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=34/32/43.406&el=135/36/10.995&scl=25000&bid=Mlink 地図（mapionより）] * [http://maps.google.com/maps?ie=UTF8&om=1&z=18&ll=34.548948,135.6003&spn=0.002041,0.003074&t=k google.mapsの衛星画像] *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/022/index.html 河内坂門原陵] - 宮内庁
{{Coord|34|32|55.84|N|135|36|1.35|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=title|name=白髪山古墳（伝清寧天皇陵）}} {{デフォルトソート:しらかやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

[[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘全景（左に前方部、右奥に後円部）}}]] '''白髪山古墳'''（しらがやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]西浦6丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の南西端に位置する。
実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内坂門原陵'''（こうちのさかどのはらのみささぎ）」として第22代[[清寧天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。
== 概要 == 白髪山古墳は、墳丘の長さ115m、前方部の幅128m・高さ11m、後円部は直径63m・高さ10.5mの前方後円墳である。 この古墳は、前方部の幅が後円部の幅の約2倍あり、前方部が大きいのが特徴である。
この古墳の名称は、被葬者とされる清寧天皇が生まれながら[[白髪]]であり、白髪大倭根子命（しらがのおおやまとねこのみこと：[[古事記]]）と名づけられていたことに由来する。
出土した円筒埴輪の破片の特徴から、[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたものと考えられている。
[[宮内庁]]によって清寧天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が前方部正面の濠の外にある。 <gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[清寧天皇]]河内坂門原陵 拝所 </gallery>
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shiragayama Kofun}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/022/index.html 河内坂門原陵] - 宮内庁
{{Coord|34|32|55.84|N|135|36|1.35|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=title|name=白髪山古墳（伝清寧天皇陵）}} {{デフォルトソート:しらかやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

[[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘全景（左に前方部、右奥に後円部）}}]] '''白髪山古墳'''（しらがやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]西浦6丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の南西端に位置する。
実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内坂門原陵'''（こうちのさかどのはらのみささぎ）」として第22代[[清寧天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。
== 概要 == 白髪山古墳は、墳丘の長さ115m、前方部の幅128m・高さ11m、後円部は直径63m・高さ10.5mの前方後円墳である。 この古墳は、前方部の幅が後円部の幅の約2倍あり、前方部が大きいのが特徴である。
この古墳の名称は、被葬者とされる清寧天皇が生まれながら[[白髪]]であり、白髪大倭根子命（しらがのおおやまとねこのみこと：[[古事記]]）と名づけられていたことに由来する。
出土した円筒埴輪の破片の特徴から、[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたものと考えられている。
[[宮内庁]]によって清寧天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が前方部正面の濠の外にある。
なお、[[福岡県]][[行橋市]]にある[[八雷古墳]]は、この白髪山古墳の設計を縮小して築造したと推定されている。<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ 行橋市ホームページ</ref> <gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[清寧天皇]]河内坂門原陵 拝所 </gallery>

{{Coord|34|32|55.84|N|135|36|1.35|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=title|name=白髪山古墳（伝清寧天皇陵）}} {{デフォルトソート:しらかやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]
[[ja:白髪山古墳]]

[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>

revision.comment: adding new pages tag to the last 100 new pages
revision.text:
[[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === <references group="注"/> === References === {{reflist}}

[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shiragiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shiragiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shiragiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shiragiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

revision.text:
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shiragiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shiragiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === <references group="注"/> === References === {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Removing this excess]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] <!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] <!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === <references group="注"/> === References === {{reflist}}

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of qq5 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and qq meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum Worship Site </gallery>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Worship Site" to "{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}}"
revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}} [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] <!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of qq5 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and qq meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A worship site is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Tumulus|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} </gallery>
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === <references group="注"/> === References === {{reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shiragayama Kofun}} *[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/022/index.html Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency

It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.

The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.

<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} </gallery>

The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} </gallery>

[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] <!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shiragiyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] <!--Shiragiyama Kofun-->[[Category:Shiragiyama Kofun]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiragiyama Kofun}}[[ja:白髪山古墳]] [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} </gallery>

The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=draft}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市|12=draft}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇|12=draft}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}} </gallery>

<!--Shiragiyama Kofun--> [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=draft}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市|12=draft}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇|12=draft}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}} </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:白髪山古墳 up to rev 87097157
revision.text:
[[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘全景（左に前方部、右奥に後円部）}}]] '''白髪山古墳'''（しらがやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]西浦6丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の南西端に位置する。
実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''河内坂門原陵'''（こうちのさかどのはらのみささぎ）」として第22代[[清寧天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。
== 概要 == [[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|白髪山古墳の空中写真}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 白髪山古墳は、墳丘の長さ115m、前方部の幅128m・高さ11m、後円部は直径63m・高さ10.5mの前方後円墳である。 この古墳は、前方部の幅が後円部の幅の約2倍あり、前方部が大きいのが特徴である。
この古墳の名称は、被葬者とされる清寧天皇が生まれながら[[白髪]]であり、白髪大倭根子命（しらがのおおやまとねこのみこと：[[古事記]]）と名づけられていたことに由来する。
出土した円筒埴輪の破片の特徴から、[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたものと考えられている。
[[宮内庁]]によって清寧天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が前方部正面の濠の外にある。
なお、[[福岡県]][[行橋市]]にある[[八雷古墳]]は、この白髪山古墳の設計を縮小して築造したと推定されている。<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ 行橋市ホームページ</ref> <gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[清寧天皇]]河内坂門原陵 拝所 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === <references group="注"/> === 出典 === {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shiragayama Kofun}} *[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/022/index.html 河内坂門原陵] - 宮内庁
{{Coord|34|32|55.84|N|135|36|1.35|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=title|name=白髪山古墳（伝清寧天皇陵）}} {{デフォルトソート:しらかやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft}} </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:大阪府の古墳
revision.text:
[[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|qq=}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|qq=}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

<!--Shiragiyama Kofun--><!--Shiragiyama Kofun--><!--Shiragiyama Kofun--><!--Shiragiyama Kofun--> [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shiragayama Kofun}}

{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|qid=Q694308}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|qid=Q357273}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11580492]] Wikidata name: Shiragayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|qid=Q694308}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|qid=Q357273}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11580492]] Wikidata name: Shiragayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|WD=Q357273}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580492]] Wikidata name: Shiragayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>
== Notes ==

<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|WD=Q357273}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580492]] Wikidata name: Shiragayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|WD=Q357273}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580492]] Wikidata name: Shiragayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>
== Notes ==

It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].

The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|WD=Q357273}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580492]] Wikidata name: Shiragayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|WD=Q357273}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580492]] Wikidata name: Shiragayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]].
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]].
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]] was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>
