revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|WD=Q357273}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|WD=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|en|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|WD=Q851668|lt=Yukuhashi, Fukuoka}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
<gallery> File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|{{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|WD=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei}} Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} </gallery>
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === <references group="注"/> === References === {{reflist}}

{{translated page|ja|白髪山古墳|version=87097157|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shiragayama Kofun]][[ja:白髪山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiragayama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.54884444|N|135.600375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Seinei|WD=Q357273|1=Emperor Seinei}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}}} [[File:Shiragayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the left, rear mound in the back right)}}]] '''Shiragayama Kofun''' (白髪山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture, Nishiura 6-chome. It is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}.
The actual burial individual is unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as "'''Kawachi Saka-no-Hara Mausoleum'''" (河内坂門原陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|WD=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei|1=Emperor Seinei}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:070727 Shiragayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Shiragayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land Image Information}}.}}]] Shiragayama Kofun has a total length of 115 meters, with the front mound measuring 128 meters in width and 11 meters in height, and the rear mound having a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters. The tumulus is characterized by its disproportionately large front mound, approximately twice as wide as the rear mound.
The name of the tumulus is derived from Emperor Seinei, who is said to have been born with {{ill|white hair|ja|白髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white hair|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒトの髪の色|1=Human hair color|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1048314}} and was named Shiraga Ōyamato Nekonomikoto (白髪大倭根子命) in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}.
Based on the characteristics of the fragments of cylindrical haniwa unearthed, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early 6th century.
The site is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, which designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Seinei. A {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located outside the moat at the front mound's central area.
Additionally, it is hypothesized that the {{ill|Yatsugura Kofun|ja|八雷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|en|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|WD=Q851668|lt=Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|1=Yukuhashi, Fukuoka}} was constructed as a scaled-down version of Shiragayama Kofun.<ref>http://www.city.yukuhashi.fukuoka.jp/doc/2015121500016/ Yukuhashi City official website</ref>
